name: Glow Protocol
subtitle: Multi-Peptide Skin Rejuvenation Complex
overview: Combination featuring BPC-157, TB-500, and GHK-Cu marketed for skin rejuvenation and anti-aging. Individual components have research support, but combination protocols require careful dosing to avoid exceeding therapeutic ranges.
mechanism: BPC-157 provides anti-inflammatory effects; TB-500 supports angiogenesis; GHK-Cu stimulates collagen production. Fixed-ratio combinations may deliver individual peptides outside optimal therapeutic ranges.
keyBenefits: Single injection delivers multiple regenerative peptides
Theoretical additive effects from combined components
Convenience factor versus individual peptide administration

items.name: Skin Rejuvenation
items.description: Theoretical synergistic effects; formal combination studies are limited

items.name: Wound Healing
items.description: BPC-157 and TB-500 components support tissue regeneration

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection to abdominal area, outer thigh, or upper arm.

steps: Clean vial top with alcohol pad

interactions.notes: Different mechanisms may complement cellular energy support
Antioxidant support compatible with peptide effects
May increase skin sensitivity; monitor for irritation
Risk of copper overdose when combining products
Total daily intake may exceed therapeutic ranges

stopSigns: Severe injection site reactions or infection

good: Proper packaging stating individual peptide amounts
warning: Verify peptide content matches supplier specifications
Calculate individual peptide doses before starting
bad: Excessive individual peptide amounts exceeding recommended ranges

references.title: Gastric pentadecapeptide BPC 157 accelerates healing of transected rat Achilles tendon and in vitro stimulates tendocytes growth
references.authors: Staresinic M, Sebecic B, Patrlj L, et al.
references.keyFindings: BPC-157 improved Achilles tendon recovery biomechanically, functionally, and microscopically; reversed HNE-mediated growth inhibition of tendocytes.

references.title: Regenerative and Protective Actions of the GHK-Cu Peptide in the Light of the New Gene Data
references.authors: Pickart L, Vasquez-Soltero JM, Margolina A
references.keyFindings: GHK-Cu modulates over 4,000 human genes, stimulates collagen, elastin, and GAG synthesis, and promotes tissue repair across skin, lung, bone, liver, and stomach.

references.title: A first-in-human, randomized, double-blind, single- and multiple-dose, phase I study of recombinant human thymosin β4 in healthy Chinese volunteers
references.authors: Wang X, Liu L, Qi L, et al.
references.journal: Journal of Cellular and Molecular Medicine
references.keyFindings: Phase I trial in 54 healthy subjects demonstrated recombinant thymosin β4 was well tolerated and safe across ascending IV doses up to 25 μg/kg with no significant accumulation.

references.title: No Published Studies on GLOW Combination
references.keyFindings: No clinical or preclinical studies examine the three-peptide GLOW combination; all evidence is extrapolated from individual component research.

latestResearch.title: Safety of Intravenous Infusion of BPC157 in Humans: A Pilot Study
latestResearch.source: Alternative Therapies in Health and Medicine
latestResearch.summary: First human IV safety study showed no adverse effects at doses up to 20 mg in healthy adults, with no impact on heart, liver, kidney, thyroid, or glucose biomarkers.

latestResearch.title: Emerging Use of BPC-157 in Orthopaedic Sports Medicine: A Systematic Review
latestResearch.summary: Systematic review of 36 studies found BPC-157 enhances GH receptor expression and angiogenesis, improving functional and biomechanical outcomes in musculoskeletal injury models.

latestResearch.title: Topically applied GHK as an anti-wrinkle peptide: Advantages, problems and prospective
latestResearch.summary: Review of GHK-Cu's tissue regeneration and collagen-stimulating properties, noting strong preclinical evidence but a surprising absence of formal clinical trials for topical anti-aging use.

quickStats.typicalDose: Per formulation instructions (typically 50mg GHK-Cu, 10mg TB-500, 10mg BPC-157 per dose)
quickStats.storage: Lyophilized: 2-8°C refrigerated; Reconstituted: 2-8°C refrigerated immediately after mixing
